title: Form 15 — Motion to Change
subtitle: Ontario Family Court — Family Law Rules (FLR 15)
helpIntro: Form 15 is used when you want to change an existing court order or agreement and the other party does NOT agree to the change. If both parties agree, use Form 23 (Consent Motion to Change) instead. To bring a Motion to Change, you must show the court there has been a 'material change in circumstances' since the original order was made — something significant has changed that was not anticipated at the time.

parts.title: Court information
parts.subtitle: Step 1 of 7
parts.intro: Enter the court file details from the original order you want to change.

fields.label: Courthouse
options: St. Catharines — Superior Court of Justice
Thunder Bay — Superior Court of Justice

fields.label: Court file number
fields.helpText: The file number from the original order. If you are registering an agreement to change, this may be a new file.

fields.label: Your full legal name (the person asking for the change)
Other party's full legal name

parts.title: The existing order or agreement
parts.subtitle: Step 2 of 7
parts.intro: Describe the existing order or agreement you want to change. Attach a copy when you file.

fields.label: What are you asking to change?
options: A combined court order and agreement

fields.label: Date of the existing order or agreement
fields.helpText: Found at the top or signature block of the original document.

fields.label: Name of the judge or officer who made the order (if a court order)
What does the existing order or agreement currently say?
fields.placeholder:
e.g. The Respondent pays child support of $950 per month for the child Alex Lance, born April 12, 2015.

The parties share decision-making responsibility for Alex. Alex lives primarily with the Applicant with the Respondent having parenting time every other weekend.
fields.helpText: Copy the relevant paragraphs from the existing order. Attach the full order when you file.

parts.title: Material change in circumstances
parts.subtitle: Step 3 of 7
parts.intro: This is the most critical part. You must convince the court that something significant has changed since the original order was made. Without a material change, the court will not reopen the matter.

fields.label: What type of change has occurred?
options: Change in income — mine has decreased significantly
Change in income — the other party's has increased significantly
Change in income — mine has increased significantly
Child's needs have changed (age, health, education, special needs)
Parenting arrangement is no longer working
Child has reached age of majority or become independent

fields.label: Describe the material change in detail
fields.placeholder: e.g. Since the order was made in March 2023, my employment circumstances have changed significantly. I was laid off from my position as a project manager in January 2026. My annual income has dropped from $95,000 to approximately $32,000 in employment insurance benefits. This change was not anticipated at the time of the original order.
fields.helpText: Be specific: what changed, when it changed, why it was not anticipated in the original order, and how it affects the existing terms. The court needs to see that this is a real, lasting change — not a temporary setback.

fields.label: When did this change occur?
fields.helpText: The approximate date the change began.

fields.label: Explain why this change was not anticipated when the original order was made
fields.placeholder: e.g. At the time of the order in March 2023, I was employed full-time and my income was stable. The layoff in January 2026 was unexpected and due to company-wide restructuring beyond my control.

parts.title: Changes you are asking for
parts.subtitle: Step 4 of 7
parts.intro: Describe exactly what you want the court to change. Be as specific as possible about the new terms you are proposing.

fields.label: Are you asking to change child support?
Current child support amount
New child support amount you are proposing
fields.helpText: Child support is set by the Child Support Guidelines based on the payor's income. Use the federal child support tables at canada.ca to find the guideline amount for your income and number of children.

fields.label: Date you want the new amount to start
fields.helpText: Courts often make changes retroactive to the date of the motion or the date circumstances changed.

fields.label: Describe the full child support change you are requesting
fields.placeholder: e.g. I am requesting that child support be reduced from $950 per month to $385 per month, which is the Child Support Guidelines amount for one child based on my current annual income of $32,000. I am requesting this change be effective January 1, 2026.

fields.label: Are you asking to change spousal support?
Current spousal support amount
New spousal support amount you are proposing (enter 0 to terminate)
Describe the spousal support change you are requesting
fields.placeholder: e.g. I am requesting that spousal support be terminated effective June 1, 2026, as the recipient has become self-sufficient, earning $68,000 per year, and the original basis for support no longer exists.

fields.label: Are you asking to change parenting time or decision-making?
Current parenting arrangement
fields.placeholder: e.g. Alex lives primarily with the Applicant. The Respondent has parenting time every other weekend from Friday at 6 p.m. to Sunday at 6 p.m.

fields.label: Proposed new parenting arrangement
fields.placeholder: e.g. I am requesting equal parenting time on a week-on, week-off schedule. Alex is now 11 years old and has expressed a strong preference to spend more time with both parents.
fields.helpText: Be specific about days, times, and holiday schedules. Always frame changes in terms of the child's best interests.

fields.label: Are you asking to change any other term of the order?
Describe the other change you are requesting
fields.placeholder: e.g. I am requesting that the requirement to maintain life insurance be removed, as the child support obligation is being reduced.

parts.title: Children
parts.subtitle: Step 5 of 7
parts.intro: If the changes involve children, provide their current details.

fields.label: Do the requested changes involve children?
List the children (name, date of birth, where they live now)
fields.placeholder:
Alex Lance — born April 12, 2015 — living with Applicant
Sam Lance — born September 3, 2018 — living with Applicant

fields.label: Describe how the children's situation has changed since the original order
fields.placeholder: e.g. Alex is now 11 years old and in Grade 6. Since the order was made, Alex has started a competitive hockey program that runs on weekends, which conflicts with the current parenting schedule.

fields.label: Why are the proposed changes in the best interests of the children?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Alex has expressed a clear preference (at age 11) to spend more time with the Respondent. Equal parenting time will strengthen Alex's relationship with both parents and is consistent with Alex's wishes.

parts.title: Temporary order (if needed urgently)
parts.subtitle: Step 6 of 7
parts.intro: If you need the court to make a temporary change right away — while your full Motion to Change is being scheduled — you can ask for a temporary order.

fields.label: Are you asking for a temporary order in addition to the final change?
fields.helpText: A temporary order provides immediate relief while you wait for the full hearing, which can be months away.

fields.label: Describe the temporary order you are asking for
fields.placeholder: e.g. I am asking for a temporary reduction in child support from $950 to $385 per month, effective immediately, until the full motion is heard. I cannot afford the current amount on my reduced income.

fields.label: Why is a temporary order needed urgently?
fields.placeholder: e.g. I have been unable to meet the current support obligation since my layoff in January 2026. I am accumulating arrears and face enforcement action by the Family Responsibility Office.

parts.title: Review and sign
parts.subtitle: Step 7 of 7
parts.intro: Review your Motion to Change. After filing, you must serve the other party and file a Form 15A (Change Information) and a supporting affidavit.

fields.label: I understand I must also file Form 15A (Change Information) with this motion.
fields.helpText: Form 15A provides the court with detailed financial information to support the change. It must be filed at the same time as Form 15.

fields.label: I understand I must serve the other party with this motion and file proof of service (Form 6B).
Date of signature
I confirm the information in this Motion to Change is accurate and complete.
